jobs.title: Influencer Marketing Coordinator - THE NORTH FACE
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: The North Face EMEA is looking for a Influencer Marketing Coordinator to join our Communications Marketing team based in Stabio, Switzerland. Live the Brand The North Face is the premier exploration company in the world. We spark curiosity. We dare to disrupt. We create communities. And we lead with integrity. We are a community of explorers and are constantly looking for new ideas, places and people to connect with. The Influencer Marketing Coordinator plays a key role in shaping and executing The North Face’s influencer strategy across EMEA. This role is responsible for managing and growing our network of talents, content creators, and cultural ambassadors, ensuring compelling, authentic storytelling that drives brand relevance and business impact. You will work collaboratively across Brand, Creative, and eComm teams to create and deliver influencer campaigns that connect The North Face with new and existing audiences. The ideal candidate is both creative and data-driven - someone who can identify talent, build long-term relationships, execute holistic campaigns, and measure performance with precision. What You Will Do Influencer Strategy & Campaign Execution - Support the development and execution of influencer marketing strategies aligned with brand and seasonal priorities. - Identify, evaluate, and onboard new creators who align with brand values across performance, outdoor, lifestyle, and cultural segments. - Coordinate influencer partnerships from briefing, contract negotiation, and product seeding through to content delivery and reporting. - Collaborate with agencies and internal teams to ensure campaigns are delivered on time, on budget, and on brand. Community & Relationship Management - Manage The North Face’s existing influencer and ambassador networks, nurturing long-term relationships built on authenticity and shared values. - Drive the evolution of the Explorer Team to broaden continuous reach and engagement. - Stay ahead of industry shifts, emerging platforms, and creator trends to continuously refresh our community mix. Project Management - End‑to‑end project management for campaigns, including timelines, deliverables, briefing, tracking progress, and ensuring all assets are delivered on time and aligned with brand expectations. - Drive cross‑functional alignment for influencer projects, ensuring milestones and campaign objectives are met through effective planning and communication. - Oversee the workflow of influencer content production. Skills for Success - Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field. - Experience: 2–4 years of experience in influencer marketing, digital marketing, or social-driven campaigns, ideally within fashion, sports, or lifestyle. - Passion for influencer culture and content creation, with strong aesthetic sensibility and brand awareness. - Analytical skills: experience in influencer management and analytics platforms as well as an analytical mindset to interpret performance data and assess campaign impact. - Skilled communicator and relationship builder who thrives in fast-paced, cross-functional environments. - Organized, detail-oriented, and proactive in managing multiple projects simultaneously. What's in it for you? - Be part of iconic lifestyle brands - Join a multi brand and multi countries organization to drive you career forward - International and challenging working culture - On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives - Discount 50% on all VF brands - Subsidized canteen as well as break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Join the North Face Family The North Face is the premier exploration company in the world. Founded in the counterculture of Berkeley, CA in 1966 we have a long and storied legacy of enabling exploration, loving and protecting wild places, and creating iconic and technically advanced product. We believe that exploration is a mindset – both on the mountain and off the mountain – and it infuses everything we do. As a community of explorers, we stay curious about new ideas, places and people. #Readytoapply R-20260123-0007

jobs.title: Global Product Developer
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF2
jobs.description: icebreaker are looking for a Global Product Developer to join the product creation team in Stabio, Switzerland. Let’s Talk About the Role As Global Product Developer , you will be accountable for the execution of icebreaker products through the GTM process from concept to finished goods, ensuring alignment with brand strategy, global business needs, sourcing capabilities, and quality standards. This role plays a critical part in the tactical execution of industry‑leading product to design intent, collaborating cross‑functionally with a multi‑season time horizon. As a key member of the product creation team, you will support and enable the Manager, Global Product Development in implementing best practices and processes, applying technical know‑how to turn product concepts into reality and drive business growth. ## ## How You Will Make a Difference ## • Concept Translation & Technical Realisation - Work closely with Design and Merchandising to interpret and validate creative concepts and align on desired outcomes (Style & GM%). - Translate concepts into comprehensive technical specifications and production strategies, from capability trials to bulk. - Ensure ideas are effectively translated into products that meet strategic and commercial objectives. ## • End-to-End Product Excellence - Prepare detailed product tech packs: specifications, materials, sizing, construction, schematics, and QC protocols. - Ensure accurate realisation of design intent in manufacturing. - Proactively identify process and manufacturing improvements, focusing on quality and finish. ## • Cross-Functional Collaboration - Act as a liaison between internal teams (Materials, Design, Technical Design, Merchandising) and external partners (Sourcing, Vendors, Quality). - Build strong working relationships to ensure alignment on strategy, timelines, and quality benchmarks. ## • Project Management - Manage timelines for seasonal and non-seasonal products, following critical paths and ensuring on‑time delivery of prototypes, SMS, and bulk. - Apply agile methodologies and troubleshoot proactively to ensure timely, high‑quality, and cost‑effective execution. - Support Quality Control in reviewing finished goods against factory standards. ## • Data Management & Reporting - Maintain the PLM system with up-to-date product data, ensuring accuracy and on-time seasonal inputs/outputs (specs, timelines, revisions, costings). - Ensure seamless information flow across Product Creation and Manufacturing teams. ## • Product Costing - Develop and manage cost models toward predetermined GM% targets. - Collaborate with Merchandising and Sourcing to meet financial parameters without compromising quality or sustainability. - Complete technical specifications enabling vendors to execute efficiently for SMS and Bulk. ## • Continuous Improvement & Feedback - Analyze market feedback and product performance to refine products and influence future development. - Seek opportunities for personal growth, with a focus on product category expertise ## Skills for Success - Ability to identify emerging trends in outdoor performance and sustainable apparel. - Use forward‑thinking to keep icebreaker at the forefront of the industry. - Strong commitment to sustainability, ethical production, attention to detail, and high-quality execution. - Solid understanding of garment construction, technical specs, and sustainable development practices. - Proficiency in detailed tech packs and PLM systems (Flex, Enovia). - Experience managing cost models in a dynamic environment. - Proven success in cross-functional teamwork. - Ability to partner effectively with Merchandising, Design, Technical Design, and external stakeholders. - Ability to articulate technical details clearly to both technical and non‑technical partners. - Confident negotiator with internal and external stakeholders. - Capable of prioritizing workload across multi‑phase projects. - Comfortable working and traveling internationally, occasionally during early mornings or evenings. - Strong time management and interpersonal skills. - Embraces an inclusive, respectful culture and handles conflict with objectivity and calm. ## Education & Experience - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Product Development, Industrial Design, or related field. - Certifications in sustainable production or project management are a plus. - Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Adobe CC , and experience with PLM systems such as Flex and Enovia. - Several years of product development/technical experience in the apparel industry; outdoor performance or sustainable apparel experience strongly preferred. ## About VF VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at http://vfc.com ## What’s in It for You - A supportive, feedback‑based culture guided by respect and integrity - An inclusive, international environment celebrating diverse backgrounds - Opportunity to be part of an iconic lifestyle brand within a multi-brand, multi‑country organization - On‑site gym with wellbeing initiatives - 50% discount on all VF brands - Break‑out areas with complimentary hot drinks Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. ## #readytoapply R-20260130-0006

descriptionByLocale.de: icebreaker are looking for a Global Product Developer to join the product creation team in Stabio, Switzerland. Let’s Talk About the Role As Global Product Developer , you will be accountable for the execution of icebreaker products through the GTM process from concept to finished goods, ensuring alignment with brand strategy, global business needs, sourcing capabilities, and quality standards. This role plays a critical part in the tactical execution of industry‑leading product to design intent, collaborating cross‑functionally with a multi‑season time horizon. As a key member of the product creation team, you will support and enable the Manager, Global Product Development in implementing best practices and processes, applying technical know‑how to turn product concepts into reality and drive business growth. ## ## How You Will Make a Difference ## • Concept Translation & Technical Realisation - Work closely with Design and Merchandising to interpret and validate creative concepts and align on desired outcomes (Style & GM%). - Translate concepts into comprehensive technical specifications and production strategies, from capability trials to bulk. - Ensure ideas are effectively translated into products that meet strategic and commercial objectives. ## • End-to-End Product Excellence - Prepare detailed product tech packs: specifications, materials, sizing, construction, schematics, and QC protocols. - Ensure accurate realisation of design intent in manufacturing. - Proactively identify process and manufacturing improvements, focusing on quality and finish. ## • Cross-Functional Collaboration - Act as a liaison between internal teams (Materials, Design, Technical Design, Merchandising) and external partners (Sourcing, Vendors, Quality). - Build strong working relationships to ensure alignment on strategy, timelines, and quality benchmarks. ## • Project Management - Manage timelines for seasonal and non-seasonal products, following critical paths and ensuring on‑time delivery of prototypes, SMS, and bulk. - Apply agile methodologies and troubleshoot proactively to ensure timely, high‑quality, and cost‑effective execution. - Support Quality Control in reviewing finished goods against factory standards. ## • Data Management & Reporting - Maintain the PLM system with up-to-date product data, ensuring accuracy and on-time seasonal inputs/outputs (specs, timelines, revisions, costings). - Ensure seamless information flow across Product Creation and Manufacturing teams. ## • Product Costing - Develop and manage cost models toward predetermined GM% targets. - Collaborate with Merchandising and Sourcing to meet financial parameters without compromising quality or sustainability. - Complete technical specifications enabling vendors to execute efficiently for SMS and Bulk. ## • Continuous Improvement & Feedback - Analyze market feedback and product performance to refine products and influence future development. - Seek opportunities for personal growth, with a focus on product category expertise ## Skills for Success - Ability to identify emerging trends in outdoor performance and sustainable apparel. - Use forward‑thinking to keep icebreaker at the forefront of the industry. - Strong commitment to sustainability, ethical production, attention to detail, and high-quality execution. - Solid understanding of garment construction, technical specs, and sustainable development practices. - Proficiency in detailed tech packs and PLM systems (Flex, Enovia). - Experience managing cost models in a dynamic environment. - Proven success in cross-functional teamwork. - Ability to partner effectively with Merchandising, Design, Technical Design, and external stakeholders. - Ability to articulate technical details clearly to both technical and non‑technical partners. - Confident negotiator with internal and external stakeholders. - Capable of prioritizing workload across multi‑phase projects. - Comfortable working and traveling internationally, occasionally during early mornings or evenings. - Strong time management and interpersonal skills. - Embraces an inclusive, respectful culture and handles conflict with objectivity and calm. ## Education & Experience - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Product Development, Industrial Design, or related field. - Certifications in sustainable production or project management are a plus. - Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Adobe CC , and experience with PLM systems such as Flex and Enovia. - Several years of product development/technical experience in the apparel industry; outdoor performance or sustainable apparel experience strongly preferred. ## About VF VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at http://vfc.com ## What’s in It for You - A supportive, feedback‑based culture guided by respect and integrity - An inclusive, international environment celebrating diverse backgrounds - Opportunity to be part of an iconic lifestyle brand within a multi-brand, multi‑country organization - On‑site gym with wellbeing initiatives - 50% discount on all VF brands - Break‑out areas with complimentary hot drinks Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. ## #readytoapply R-20260130-0006

jobs.title: PSS Analyst - THE NORTH FACE
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: The North Face EMEA is looking for an outstanding PSS Analyst to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland. Focus of this role will be on Retail Partner and Concession channel, with the key objective to create and implement new ways of working to elevate our Merchandise Planning and Trading aligned with the DTC business. You will be responsible for merchandise trading including all inventory management and Retail KPIs management and cross-functional go-to-market alignment and performance tracking. Also, you will play a key role to further strengthen our Retail Partner business in ITALY and GAS region, working closely with our Retail and Concession manager throughout the selling campaign. How you will make a difference - Lead and evolve best-in-class digital Merchandising Planning process fully aligned with our DTC strategy, positioning and with the seasonal merchandising strategy; - Full ownership to manage the performance of the defined assortment, working closely with a cross-functional team dedicated to B&M (Buyers and Retail Partner managers) ; - Work alongside our TNG buying team and PSS managers to manage seasonal, monthly and weekly intake requirements; - Working on Inventory Management and coverage optimization at product/size level and across our DCs, with focus on trading performance and stock level optimization, delivering against The North Face B&M and financial objectives; - Full alignment with overall go-to-market calendar to ensure seamless execution and strong accelerated growth; - Working in partnership with Buying, PSS and Sales Ops team on the development of yearly and seasonal merchandise financial plans across Revenue, COGS, Discounts, Margins, ASP and Units - Set standards of trading and buying processes and deliver best practices to be shared across our cross-functional teams, proactively proposing continuous improvements in processes and tools in collaboration with DTC counterparts and VF Central Team to reach trading excellence and best-in-class inventory management execution; - Constantly striving for innovation and to optimise ways of working by contributing in setting up new processes, tools and report. What you need to succeed? - 3+ yrs. of relevant work experience with proven track record in Merchandising Planning, trading or equivalent Business Planning/Trading role - Language skills: fluent in English and Italian - Relevant Merchandise Planning and analysis experience within a fast-paced retail business - Deep understanding of the fast-paced B&M environment and merchandising success factors for Retails - Outstanding understanding of all the Retail KPIs and how they are influencing stores’ P&L - Excellent knowledge of Excel. Knowledge of Access, SAP and BI systems is a plus. - Excellent analytical skills - Outstanding business- and financial acumen - Consumer-first, data savvy and brand-driven mindset - Highly performance driven and result-oriented team player who is getting things done - High maturity in managing organisational complexity and effective communication - Strategic and operational decision-making A formal education and subsequent University Bachelor or Master’s degree in related fields are nice to have, but we are most interested in your total experience and professional achievements. What is in it for you? - Playing a key role to accelerate our B&M growth within one of our leading brands - Joining an ambitious Retail and Concession team embedded into The North Face and VF, one of the largest, globally leading multi-brand organisations with great long-term career opportunities - Purpose-led, international, fun and challenging working environment We're in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That's why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we're the total package. - A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do - An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together - Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization - On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives - 50% discount on all VF brands Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose-led, performance driven company, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Join the North Face Family The North Face was built on a love for the outdoors and the desire to enable all types of exploration, for all people, from their backyards to the Himalayas. Over the last 50 years we've lived by our "true north," the belief that exploration has the power to change us, to challenge us and to help us see the world from new perspectives. To help ignite a global movement of outdoor exploration, love for the natural world and an unwavering commitment to protect it, we welcome and encourage new participants to the outdoors. We extend our reach through support of non-profit organizations, establishing programs that connect participants with close-to-home recreation opportunities, and creating a connection with young leaders, inspiring them to explore and conserve. As we enter our sixth decade, we pledge to: - Empower exploration and the thrill of the unknown for as many people as possible - Protect the places where we live, play, and operate - Evolve the way we make our products by improving our environmental performance and social responsibility in the supply chain Our Parent Company, VF Corporation VF is one of the world's largest apparel, footwear and accessories companies connecting people to the lifestyles, activities and experiences they cherish through our family of iconic outdoor, active and workwear brands. At the heart of our journey lies our company-wide purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It's the reason we come to work every day. It's a commitment shared by our global associates across all brands. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you! Please submit your application in English, as that's our common language! R-20230303-0011

It's the reason we come to work every day. It's a commitment shared by our global associates across all brands. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you! Please submit your application in English, as that's our common language! R-20230303-0011
descriptionByLocale.fr: The North Face EMEA is looking for an outstanding PSS Analyst to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland. Focus of this role will be on Retail Partner and Concession channel, with the key objective to create and implement new ways of working to elevate our Merchandise Planning and Trading aligned with the DTC business. You will be responsible for merchandise trading including all inventory management and Retail KPIs management and cross-functional go-to-market alignment and performance tracking. Also, you will play a key role to further strengthen our Retail Partner business in ITALY and GAS region, working closely with our Retail and Concession manager throughout the selling campaign. How you will make a difference - Lead and evolve best-in-class digital Merchandising Planning process fully aligned with our DTC strategy, positioning and with the seasonal merchandising strategy; - Full ownership to manage the performance of the defined assortment, working closely with a cross-functional team dedicated to B&M (Buyers and Retail Partner managers) ; - Work alongside our TNG buying team and PSS managers to manage seasonal, monthly and weekly intake requirements; - Working on Inventory Management and coverage optimization at product/size level and across our DCs, with focus on trading performance and stock level optimization, delivering against The North Face B&M and financial objectives; - Full alignment with overall go-to-market calendar to ensure seamless execution and strong accelerated growth; - Working in partnership with Buying, PSS and Sales Ops team on the development of yearly and seasonal merchandise financial plans across Revenue, COGS, Discounts, Margins, ASP and Units - Set standards of trading and buying processes and deliver best practices to be shared across our cross-functional teams, proactively proposing continuous improvements in processes and tools in collaboration with DTC counterparts and VF Central Team to reach trading excellence and best-in-class inventory management execution; - Constantly striving for innovation and to optimise ways of working by contributing in setting up new processes, tools and report. What you need to succeed? - 3+ yrs. of relevant work experience with proven track record in Merchandising Planning, trading or equivalent Business Planning/Trading role - Language skills: fluent in English and Italian - Relevant Merchandise Planning and analysis experience within a fast-paced retail business - Deep understanding of the fast-paced B&M environment and merchandising success factors for Retails - Outstanding understanding of all the Retail KPIs and how they are influencing stores’ P&L - Excellent knowledge of Excel. Knowledge of Access, SAP and BI systems is a plus. - Excellent analytical skills - Outstanding business- and financial acumen - Consumer-first, data savvy and brand-driven mindset - Highly performance driven and result-oriented team player who is getting things done - High maturity in managing organisational complexity and effective communication - Strategic and operational decision-making A formal education and subsequent University Bachelor or Master’s degree in related fields are nice to have, but we are most interested in your total experience and professional achievements. What is in it for you? - Playing a key role to accelerate our B&M growth within one of our leading brands - Joining an ambitious Retail and Concession team embedded into The North Face and VF, one of the largest, globally leading multi-brand organisations with great long-term career opportunities - Purpose-led, international, fun and challenging working environment We're in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That's why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we're the total package. - A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do - An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together - Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization - On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives - 50% discount on all VF brands Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose-led, performance driven company, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Join the North Face Family The North Face was built on a love for the outdoors and the desire to enable all types of exploration, for all people, from their backyards to the Himalayas. Over the last 50 years we've lived by our "true north," the belief that exploration has the power to change us, to challenge us and to help us see the world from new perspectives. To help ignite a global movement of outdoor exploration, love for the natural world and an unwavering commitment to protect it, we welcome and encourage new participants to the outdoors. We extend our reach through support of non-profit organizations, establishing programs that connect participants with close-to-home recreation opportunities, and creating a connection with young leaders, inspiring them to explore and conserve. As we enter our sixth decade, we pledge to: - Empower exploration and the thrill of the unknown for as many people as possible - Protect the places where we live, play, and operate - Evolve the way we make our products by improving our environmental performance and social responsibility in the supply chain Our Parent Company, VF Corporation VF is one of the world's largest apparel, footwear and accessories companies connecting people to the lifestyles, activities and experiences they cherish through our family of iconic outdoor, active and workwear brands. At the heart of our journey lies our company-wide purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It's the reason we come to work every day. It's a commitment shared by our global associates across all brands. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you! Please submit your application in English, as that's our common language! R-20230303-0011

jobs.title: Retail Auditor Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Retail Auditor, Intern for Finance Team. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Are you interested in supporting a Retail Audit team and taking on an exciting challenge within the dynamic store environment? In this role, you will: - Support database preparation for the launch of new claim procedures. - Contribute to AI literacy by supporting the integration of AI concepts into existing processes and procedures to improve efficiency where possible. - Audit inventory movements , ensuring accuracy between store records and accounting data. - Assist team members with their daily operational and audit activities. How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - Fluency in English , spoken as well as written; any other European language like French or German is a plus - Strong working knowledge of computer systems, especially Microsoft Office and Outlook , with a dvanced Excel skills being essential. Exposure to AI tools is a plus. - Excellent planning, analytical, and organizational skills - Ability to work independently while also collaborating effectively within a team What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Learning & Stock Monitoring : Support daily inventory activities by learning how to monitor stock levels and reporting any immediate discrepancies. - Data Collection & Analysis Support : Assist the team in gathering data to investigate inventory mismatches and help map out potential root causes (e.g., entry errors or damages). - Operational Support for Counts : Actively participate in periodic stock takes and cycle counts, ensuring physical counts match system records under supervision. - Cross-functional Collaboration : Support information flow between Supply Chain, Logistics, and Warehouse teams to maintain data accuracy. - Basic Reporting & Dashboards : Help prepare simple inventory reports and update tracking dashboards (e.g., using Excel or internal tools). - Process Contribution : Provide "fresh-eye" observations to identify small operational bottlenecks and suggest simple improvements to daily workflows. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership In this internship, you’ll gain hands-on experience in how seasonal assortments are built and brought to life across the European market. You’ll work closely with experienced merchandisers, gaining exposure to product strategy, consumer insights, and cross-functional collaboration. We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260327-0005

jobs.title: Associate, Merchandising Operations (Timberland)
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Timberland EMEA is looking for an exceptional Associate, Merchandising Operations on a 6‑month fixed‑term contract , to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland. ## Let’s Talk about the Role As Associate, Merchandising Operations , you will support and partially own key operational activities across Global Merchandising, Global Product, Regional Merchandising, Planning, Pricing and Commercial teams. You will contribute to ensuring accuracy, consistency and timely delivery of Global Merchandising deliverables, while helping streamline templates, calendars, systems and processes to improve efficiency across the merchandising organization. You will contribute by: • Enovia/PLM System Coordination - Maintain and monitor data accuracy within Enovia/PLM across categories. - Act as a point of contact for basic-to-intermediate PLM issues, ensuring timely escalation and resolution with the Merch Ops Manager and system teams. - Support system enhancements and new tool/process adoption (UAT testing, documentation, data updates). - Identify recurring issues or gaps and propose improvements to data flows or operational routines. • Line Plan Communication and Management - Coordinate pre‑season and in‑season distribution of line lists, ensuring updates reach all cross‑functional partners according to calendar. - Collaborate with Global Merchandising, Regional Merchandising, Planning, Pricing, Master Data and Commercial teams to ensure product information accuracy and timely system flow into SAP. - Support troubleshooting for SKU‑level issues and syndication delays, driving first-level analysis before escalation. • Merchandising Operations & Process Support - Maintain and update global merchandising calendars, templates and timelines. - Help ensure process adherence across merchant teams by monitoring deadlines and tracking key milestones. - Prepare and maintain process documentation, input materials, dashboards and reports used by the Merch Ops function. - Contribute to identifying areas for process improvement and support implementation of new ways of working. - Represent Merch Ops in selected cross-functional meetings when required, ensuring clear communication of deliverables and follow-ups. • Merch Analytics & Reporting - Support category and merchandising teams with ad‑hoc reports, structured data pulls and preliminary insights. - Coordinate catalogue development tasks, ensuring timely collection and validation of product information. - Maintain updated merchandising databases and ensure accessibility of information for internal and external stakeholders. Skills for Success The ideal Associate, Merch Ops has strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and a collaborative mindset, with growing autonomy and confidence in managing operational workflows. - A few years of experience in Merch Ops, Merchandising, Planning, Business Operations or similar roles - Strong Excel skills and good understanding of PLM systems - Solid understanding of GTM process and basic retail/wholesale mechanics - Familiarity with merchandising planning concepts and product creation is a strong plus - Ability to organize information, manage multiple deadlines and support decision‑making - Strong communication and stakeholder management skills - Fluent in English (written and verbal) - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, Apparel or related fields ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose‑led, performance‑driven company, we foster a culture of belonging based on respect, openness, connection, and authenticity. We celebrate diversity and support equal opportunities for all associates and applicants. If this sounds like you and you’re ready to join our team, we’d love to hear from you! #readytoapply R-20260309-0029

descriptionByLocale.en: Timberland EMEA is looking for an exceptional Associate, Merchandising Operations on a 6‑month fixed‑term contract , to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland. ## Let’s Talk about the Role As Associate, Merchandising Operations , you will support and partially own key operational activities across Global Merchandising, Global Product, Regional Merchandising, Planning, Pricing and Commercial teams. You will contribute to ensuring accuracy, consistency and timely delivery of Global Merchandising deliverables, while helping streamline templates, calendars, systems and processes to improve efficiency across the merchandising organization. You will contribute by: • Enovia/PLM System Coordination - Maintain and monitor data accuracy within Enovia/PLM across categories. - Act as a point of contact for basic-to-intermediate PLM issues, ensuring timely escalation and resolution with the Merch Ops Manager and system teams. - Support system enhancements and new tool/process adoption (UAT testing, documentation, data updates). - Identify recurring issues or gaps and propose improvements to data flows or operational routines. • Line Plan Communication and Management - Coordinate pre‑season and in‑season distribution of line lists, ensuring updates reach all cross‑functional partners according to calendar. - Collaborate with Global Merchandising, Regional Merchandising, Planning, Pricing, Master Data and Commercial teams to ensure product information accuracy and timely system flow into SAP. - Support troubleshooting for SKU‑level issues and syndication delays, driving first-level analysis before escalation. • Merchandising Operations & Process Support - Maintain and update global merchandising calendars, templates and timelines. - Help ensure process adherence across merchant teams by monitoring deadlines and tracking key milestones. - Prepare and maintain process documentation, input materials, dashboards and reports used by the Merch Ops function. - Contribute to identifying areas for process improvement and support implementation of new ways of working. - Represent Merch Ops in selected cross-functional meetings when required, ensuring clear communication of deliverables and follow-ups. • Merch Analytics & Reporting - Support category and merchandising teams with ad‑hoc reports, structured data pulls and preliminary insights. - Coordinate catalogue development tasks, ensuring timely collection and validation of product information. - Maintain updated merchandising databases and ensure accessibility of information for internal and external stakeholders. Skills for Success The ideal Associate, Merch Ops has strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and a collaborative mindset, with growing autonomy and confidence in managing operational workflows. - A few years of experience in Merch Ops, Merchandising, Planning, Business Operations or similar roles - Strong Excel skills and good understanding of PLM systems - Solid understanding of GTM process and basic retail/wholesale mechanics - Familiarity with merchandising planning concepts and product creation is a strong plus - Ability to organize information, manage multiple deadlines and support decision‑making - Strong communication and stakeholder management skills - Fluent in English (written and verbal) - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, Apparel or related fields ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose‑led, performance‑driven company, we foster a culture of belonging based on respect, openness, connection, and authenticity. We celebrate diversity and support equal opportunities for all associates and applicants. If this sounds like you and you’re ready to join our team, we’d love to hear from you! #readytoapply R-20260309-0029

jobs.title: Developer, Apparel
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Timberland EMEA is looking for an exceptional Developer, Apparel , to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland. ## Let’s Talk about the Role As a key member of our Product Development team, you will provide insights and suggestions and share information to deliver quality products on time at the right cost for the intended performance use. Your work will be performed under general guidance and direction, which may require the initiation of contacts to resolve and discuss mutual problems or collaboration on procedures or transactions. Infrequent overseas development travel may be necessary. ## How You Will Make a Difference - Develop product across multiple genders by product category depending on the seasonal style count and expertise. - Participate meetings and execute against the Product expectations for product quality, cost (predictive costing) - Own Product specifications, measurements, BOM, Fit creations and maintenance. - Communicate effectively and clearly with Asia sourcing & vendors, TD/ GPC / GSP - Collaborate w/ design & merch to support the line plan through product execution and signing of for bulk production. - Hit required product creation milestones and execute on strategy - Work w/ Global Product costing team in VFA and internal merch/ design teams on seasonal FOBs to support Gross margin growth. - Be an engaged and committed team-mate, Be a facilitator in negotiations. ## ## Skills for Success The foundation skills you will need in this position are: - The ability to work with cross-functional teams, including Consumer Research, Research and Development to compile and evaluate research on future product requirements and identify enhancements to current products - The aptitude to help the Product Development team establish strategies ranging from product pricing, design, marketing, advertising, packaging, and future products to manage the product family's life - An understanding and knowledge of Timberland’s principles, practices, and procedures - The resourcefulness to determine a course of action based on guidelines and modify processes and methods as required - The capability to resolve issues which are often varied and non-routine - Apply previous experience to identify the most appropriate option or improve existing approaches - Seek clarification when needed - Undertake analysis and investigation to solve differing, but related, problems - The talent to convey complex information to occasionally divergent audiences that may require some persuasion - In some instances, audiences are not knowledgeable of the subject matter - Or an objective review of difficult work problems, obtaining cooperation or approval ## While formal education in a related field is great to have, we are most interested in a few years of experience and professional achievements. ## What’s in it For You - A supportive, feedback‑based culture guided by respect and integrity. - An inclusive, international environment where diverse backgrounds and experiences are valued. - The opportunity to be part of an iconic lifestyle brand within a global, multi‑brand organization. - On‑site gym with health and well‑being initiatives. - A discount card offering 50% off all VF brands. - Break‑out areas with complimentary hot drinks. About Timberland At Timberland, we’re a force of nature. We’re a team that steps outside, works hard together, and moves the world forward. The brand is widely recognized as the arbiter of boot culture. With a rich heritage of craftsmanship and an eye toward the future, Timberland has a decades-long commitment to make products responsibly, protect the outdoors, and build community. ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose‑led, performance‑driven company, we foster a culture of belonging based on respect, openness, connection, and authenticity. We celebrate diversity and support equal opportunities for all associates and applicants. If this sounds like you and you’re ready to join our team, we’d love to hear from you! R-20260323-0023

jobs.title: Analyst, FP&A
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional FP&A Analyst , Shared Functions to join our Finance team based in Stabio, Switzerland. Let’s talk about the role! As FP&A Analyst, will support brand FP&A activities related to P&L Planning and Forecasting focusing on Sales & Margin of Wholesale channel, playing a key role to create alignment around Gross-To-Net, analysing the main channel and accounts dynamics and performances as well as supporting the brand to make the right decisions to maximize marginality. You will gather information on forecast & plan data, which has to be loaded in the reporting tool (BPC) and deliver reports, as determined by area leads and Senior Management. To achieve the goals of the department you will work closely together with your colleagues of the Finance department as well as other functions such as DTC, Planning, Sales and report directly to the Director, FP&A. We expect that you will deliver unbeatable results whilst fulfilling the following aspects of the FP&A Analyst role: - Manage and handle Financial GTN planning and Forecasting tools and ensure consistency and accuracy of figures submitted in the systems; - Ensure that actuals are aligned to expectations and proactively analyse and report variances vs forecast with - Keep continuous alignment between financial & commercial GTN to ensure alignment/balance between Sales, Margin and inventory targets; - Deliver forecasting accuracy excellence and pro-actively analyze any critical trends or dynamics within the channels and the main accounts; - Ensure that all recurring requests related to forecast and plan are delivered accurately and on time: store roll, key accounts details, revenue walk, margin bridge, category overviews, etc.; - Support the 5YR rolling financial plan (LRP) partnering with the global strategy, global merch planning & Ops and Product. Understand the brand strategy and support in drawing conclusions through financial analysis; - Assist in ad-hoc analysis on request of European or Global/Corporate management Skills for Success You will be a professional with proven experience in business controlling and financial planning, with a strong analytical mindset and: - Minimum bachelor’s degree in finance area (accounting, controlling) and 1/2 years of related work experience preferrable; - Solid software and PC skills (Advanced in Excel, PowerPoint, SAP/COGNOS, BPC experience is a plus) - Great time management and prioritizing skills with the ability to meet strict deadlines - Strong English command (written and verbal) - Proven communication skills in interacting with people from a non-financial background - Excellent organizational skills & goal-oriented About VF VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com What’s in it For You We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package. - A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do - An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together - Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization - On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives - A discount card with 50% on all VF brands - Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. R-20260323-0032

titleByLocale.en: Analyst, FP&A
titleByLocale.it: Analista, FP&A
titleByLocale.fr: Analyste, FP&A

descriptionByLocale.en: VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional FP&A Analyst , Shared Functions to join our Finance team based in Stabio, Switzerland. Let’s talk about the role! As FP&A Analyst, will support brand FP&A activities related to P&L Planning and Forecasting focusing on Sales & Margin of Wholesale channel, playing a key role to create alignment around Gross-To-Net, analysing the main channel and accounts dynamics and performances as well as supporting the brand to make the right decisions to maximize marginality. You will gather information on forecast & plan data, which has to be loaded in the reporting tool (BPC) and deliver reports, as determined by area leads and Senior Management. To achieve the goals of the department you will work closely together with your colleagues of the Finance department as well as other functions such as DTC, Planning, Sales and report directly to the Director, FP&A. We expect that you will deliver unbeatable results whilst fulfilling the following aspects of the FP&A Analyst role: - Manage and handle Financial GTN planning and Forecasting tools and ensure consistency and accuracy of figures submitted in the systems; - Ensure that actuals are aligned to expectations and proactively analyse and report variances vs forecast with - Keep continuous alignment between financial & commercial GTN to ensure alignment/balance between Sales, Margin and inventory targets; - Deliver forecasting accuracy excellence and pro-actively analyze any critical trends or dynamics within the channels and the main accounts; - Ensure that all recurring requests related to forecast and plan are delivered accurately and on time: store roll, key accounts details, revenue walk, margin bridge, category overviews, etc.; - Support the 5YR rolling financial plan (LRP) partnering with the global strategy, global merch planning & Ops and Product. Understand the brand strategy and support in drawing conclusions through financial analysis; - Assist in ad-hoc analysis on request of European or Global/Corporate management Skills for Success You will be a professional with proven experience in business controlling and financial planning, with a strong analytical mindset and: - Minimum bachelor’s degree in finance area (accounting, controlling) and 1/2 years of related work experience preferrable; - Solid software and PC skills (Advanced in Excel, PowerPoint, SAP/COGNOS, BPC experience is a plus) - Great time management and prioritizing skills with the ability to meet strict deadlines - Strong English command (written and verbal) - Proven communication skills in interacting with people from a non-financial background - Excellent organizational skills & goal-oriented About VF VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com What’s in it For You We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package. - A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do - An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together - Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization - On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives - A discount card with 50% on all VF brands - Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. R-20260323-0032

jobs.title: Allocator Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Allocator Intern for Vans. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! We are looking for a Allocator intern to join VANS within VF EMEA Internship Program. In this role, you will provide support to the Allocation Team: - By taking an active role in analysing performance and sales trend. - Monitoring deliveries, maintaining adequate stock levels. - Taking care of replenishment / initial allocation for O&O EMEA Stores. - Developing and maintaining reports and tools finding the best way to make them efficient and useful for the team. How you will make a difference You should be an analytical, resilient and curious team player, being: - Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills. - Passionate for numbers with strong analytical skills: accuracy and attention to details is a must. - Proficient in Microsoft Excel. Knowledge of any BI platform will be considered as a plus (eg: Power BI). - Eager to learn and face up new challenges. - Proactive in order to achieve yours and your team goals. - First experiences such as other internships, extracurricular activities, is considered as a plus What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Learn about Vans’ trends and main logics of Retail Merchandising. - Learn logics of inventory management, sales analysis, and allocation strategies. - Learn to work with a wide team of internal stakeholder. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260205-0007

jobs.title: Retail Marketing Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Retail Marketing Intern for Vans. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! This internship offers hands-on experiences at the intersection of marketing and retail, where you’ll help shape in-store activations while learning how brand strategy comes to life at the point of sale Support the planning and execution of retail marketing initiatives that enhance in-store visibility, customer engagement, and sales performance. Gain experience analyzing results, coordinating with cross-functional teams, and translating brand strategy into impactful retail activations. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Retail Marketing intern: - Support the planning and execution of retail marketing and in-store activations, helping bring Vans’ brand, culture, and stories to life in stores. - Coordinate with cross-functional teams (Retail, Creative, Visual Merchandising, and other Marketing functions) to gather all tactics needed to deliver engaging in-store events and consumer activations. - Assist with retail marketing operations across EMEA, supporting different store formats (Owned, Partner, Outlet). - Contribute to training and communication materials for store teams ahead of key activations or events. - Help execute retail campaigns and store activations, including in-store visuals, digital retail communications, and event-related materials. - Provide day-to-day support on cross-functional needs, such as copy coordination, translations, and event logistics. - Work closely with DTC (Direct To Consumer) Retail teams (district and store managers) to support smooth execution at store level. - Support the creation of briefs and post-activation recaps, capturing learnings and best practices for future in-store initiatives. - Support basic budget tracking and purchase order follow-up in coordination with the team. Skills that will make you successful You are a natural facilitator who helps ideas turn into real, on-the-ground experiences: - Project-minded and well-organized – You enjoy working in project mode, juggling multiple activations, and coordinating across Creative, Retail, Visual Merchandising, and Marketing teams. - Curious about retail marketing and in-store activations – You’re eager to understand how brand storytelling, consumer experience, and store execution come together in a physical retail environment. - Culturally connected – You have a strong eye for engaging content and a genuine affinity with action sports, street culture, and the Vans DNA (skate, music, art, and self-expression). - Detail-driven yet hands-on – You care about quality, follow through on execution, and take pride in getting the small details right for events and store activations. - Proactive and solution-oriented -You show initiative, adapt quickly, and keep things moving even when plans evolve or challenges arise. - Collaborative by nature – You thrive in team environments, share information openly, and contribute positively to collective success. - Comfortable working with data and tools – You enjoy using tools like Excel and PowerPoint to track projects, support reporting, and tell clear, visual stories. - A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Communication, Business, or a related field is nice to have, but we are most interested in your mindset, curiosity, and relevant experiences. What’s in it for you By joining the Vans Retail Marketing team, you’ll gain real, hands-on experience working on in-store activations and events for an iconic global action sports brand. During your internship, you will: - Work closely with international and cross-functional teams across Retail, Marketing, Creative, and Visual Merchandising. - Contribute to real retail activations and in-store events, from idea to execution. - Learn how brand strategy translates into physical retail experiences that engage consumers. - Receive exposure to retail marketing operations across EMEA, including different store formats. - Benefit from on-the-job learning, mentoring, and collaboration with experienced professionals in the industry. By the end of the internship, you can expect to have a clear understanding of: - How retail marketing and in-store activations are planned, executed, and measured. - How cross-functional teams collaborate to deliver impactful store experiences. - How Vans’ brand values and action-sports culture are expressed at retail level. - The operational side of retail marketing, including asset production, timelines, and budget basics. - What it takes to successfully deliver consumer-facing events in a fast-paced retail environment. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260206-0013

About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Vans is looking for a curious and driven CRM & Loyalty Intern to support how we engage, reward, and grow our consumer community across EMEA, based in Stabio, Switzerland. Support the execution of Vans’ CRM and Loyalty strategy across EMEA, contributing to how we engage our community, strengthen loyalty, and bring the Vans brand to life through meaningful, data‑driven consumer experiences. You will gain hands‑on exposure to lifecycle marketing, loyalty mechanics, and cross‑functional collaboration within a global, youth‑culture‑driven brand. - Support the execution of CRM campaigns across email, eCommerce, and other consumer touchpoints, ensuring brand consistency and flawless delivery - Contribute to the setup, monitoring, and optimization of loyalty activations to drive engagement, retention, and member value - Manage and update CRM assets such as campaign calendars, briefs, tracking files, and dashboards to support day‑to‑day execution - Be responsible for supporting reporting and post‑campaign analysis, helping translate consumer and performance data into clear insights - Develop a strong understanding of the Vans consumer, community mindset, and lifecycle stages by working closely with EMEA and Central teams - Support additional ad-hoc digital media initiatives and projects as part of the wider Vans Brand Planning team How you will make a difference What will make you successful in this role: - A strong passion for the Vans brand, youth culture, and community‑driven marketing, with curiosity about how brands build long‑term relationships - A genuine interest in CRM, loyalty programs, and data‑informed decision‑making, beyond just campaign execution - An analytical mindset, with the ability to connect performance data to consumer behavior and brand impact - High attention to detail and structure, essential for managing multiple campaigns and stakeholders in parallel - A proactive, hands‑on attitude, comfortable working in a fast‑moving, international environment and learning by doing - A degree in Marketing, Business, Economics, Data, or a related field is nice to have What’s in it for you In the role of CRM & Loyalty Intern , you will work at the heart of Vans’ consumer engagement strategy, gaining hands‑on experience in how a global youth‑culture brand builds long‑term relationships with its community. By joining our team, you will: - Gain practical experience in CRM, loyalty, and lifecycle marketing , working on real campaigns that reach millions of consumers across EMEA - Learn how to translate consumer data and insights into meaningful actions that drive engagement, retention, and brand love - Develop a strong understanding of Vans Family , loyalty mechanics, and how community‑driven brands create value beyond transactions - Work closely with an international, cross‑functional team , collaborating with Brand, Digital, eCommerce, and Central CRM teams - Build confidence using industry‑leading tools and processes , with exposure to campaign execution, reporting, and performance optimization - Benefit from a supportive learning environment, including on‑the‑job coaching, feedback, and networking opportunities across teams and brands within VF This internship is a unique opportunity to learn by doing, grow your skill set, and contribute to shaping how Vans connects with its community — always "Off The Wall". The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260206-0012

jobs.title: Product Merchandising Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Product Merchandising Intern for The North Face . About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! As a Product Merchandising Intern you will support the end-to-end product lifecycle by translating market insights into compelling assortments that drive sales and brand growth. Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure the right product is in the right place at the right time, maximizing customer impact and business performance. - Support the SMU (Special Make Up) line planning process by analyzing consumer insights, sales performance, and market trends to inform the FW/SS assortment strategy for Europe. - Coordinate the go-to-market process by managing timelines, tools, and product data accuracy to ensure seamless cross-functional alignment. - Monitor in-season product performance to identify risks and opportunities, providing actionable recommendations to optimize sell-through and margin. - Collaborate with global and regional teams to ensure correct data flows into the systems & assortments reflect brand direction while meeting local market needs and commercial goals. - Contribute to visual line reviews and product presentations by supporting the preparation of compelling tools. - Support data entry of PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) systems, support with transfer from PLM to Flex and update linelists to reflect correct information How you will make a difference To succeed as an Associate Product Merchandising Intern, you should be curious about products, eager to learn how assortments are built, and ready to support a fast-paced team with energy and precision. We are looking for someone who has demonstrated: - The ability to work with numbers and draw simple conclusions to support product decisions. - The capability to stay organized and manage multiple tasks with attention to detail. - A team player. Working x functionally is essential as a assistant product merchandiser. - An understanding of what makes a product appealing to consumers , especially in the European market. - Eye for detail to ensure accuracy across the tools & PLM systems - A basic knowledge of Excel or similar tools to help with data and product tracking. - A keen interest in fashion, retail, or product creation , and a desire to grow in this field. - A positive, can-do attitude , and a willingness to support the team wherever needed. What’s in it For You By joining our team, you will: - Learn how to build and manage a seasonal product line , from concept to market launch. - Gain exposure to real-time business decisions , using data to support product and commercial strategies. - Understand the role of merchandising within a global brand , and how it connects with marketing, sales, and product teams. - Develop your analytical and storytelling skills , turning numbers into product narratives. - Build a strong professional network , with opportunities to connect across teams, functions, and brands. - Receive mentorship and training , helping you grow your confidence and capabilities in a dynamic, international environment. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership In this internship, you’ll gain hands-on experience in how seasonal assortments are built and brought to life across the European market. You’ll work closely with experienced merchandisers, gaining exposure to product strategy, consumer insights, and cross-functional collaboration. We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260209-0020
